blueprint.name: Smart Lights
blueprint.description: Automatically turns lights on and off based on binary sensors (motion sensors, door sensors, etc.), with optional adaptive lighting that adjusts brightness and color temperature based on the sun's position.
input.lights.name: Lights
input.lights.description: The lights to control: a single light, a group of lights, or even a Home Assistant area or label.
input.sensors.name: Turn-on and turn-off sensors
input.sensors.description: Sensors that drive automatic light turn-on and turn-off: works with motion and presence sensors, doors and windows, or any other type of binary sensor. Leave empty to use adaptive lighting only when the lights turn on.
input.off_delay.name: Delay before turn-off
input.off_delay.description: Delay before turning the lights off when the sensors selected above become inactive (no more motion, door closed, etc.). If several sensors are selected, the delay only starts when all sensors are inactive.
input.keep_on_open_doors.name: Keep the light on with open doors
input.keep_on_open_doors.description: By default, door sensors turn the light on when they open, but do not keep it on, and the lights turn off after the configured delay. When this option is enabled, door sensors are treated like the other types and the lights stay on as long as the doors remain open.
input.switch_entity.name: Turn-on with switch
input.switch_entity.description: The input helper entity selected here turns the lights on and off regardless of the situation. Useful for working with a physical switch: another automation can toggle this helper to turn the lights on and off. Its state is also automatically synchronized with the lights.
input.control_entities_off.name: Control entities
input.control_entities_off.description: The selected input helpers must be disabled for automatic turn-on and turn-off to work. This can be used to disable the automation when nobody is home, for example.
section.adaptive.settings.description: Optional light adaptation throughout the day.
input.enable_adaptive.name: Enable adaptive lighting
input.enable_adaptive.description: Automatically adjusts brightness and color temperature according to the sun's position throughout the day.
input.brightness_night.name: Minimum brightness
input.brightness_night.description: Minimum brightness around sunrise and sunset.
input.brightness_day.name: Maximum brightness
input.brightness_day.description: Maximum brightness around the middle of the day.
input.kelvin_night.name: Minimum temperature
input.kelvin_night.description: Warmest white temperature around sunrise and sunset.
input.kelvin_day.name: Maximum temperature
input.kelvin_day.description: Coolest white temperature around the middle of the day.
input.night_adaptive_enabled.name: Specific settings at night
input.night_adaptive_enabled.description: By default, the minimum values defined above are applied at night. Enable this option to use the following values instead.
input.night_mode_brightness.name: Night-specific brightness
input.night_mode_brightness.description: Brightness applied at night when the Specific settings at night option is enabled.
input.night_mode_kelvin.name: Night-specific temperature
input.night_mode_kelvin.description: White temperature applied at night when the Specific settings at night option is enabled.
input.auto_mode_boolean.name: Control entity
input.auto_mode_boolean.description: The selected input helper must be active for adaptive lighting to run. When it is disabled, the lights keep their last setting and are still turned on and off automatically according to the settings above. Useful for stopping adaptive lighting without disabling the other automation features.
input.restore_on_off.name: Automatic re-activation
input.restore_on_off.description: If this option is enabled, the control entity above is automatically re-enabled when all managed lights turn off.
input.disable_on_color.name: Disable on color mode
input.disable_on_color.description: If this option is enabled, the control entity above is automatically disabled when one of the managed lights switches to color mode.
input.weather_entity.name: Adjust with the weather
input.weather_entity.description: Optional weather entity used to slightly adjust adaptive brightness and white temperature according to the weather forecast.
input.auto_on_mode.name: Automatic turn-on
input.auto_on_mode.description: Choose when automatic turn-on should be active. Night mode uses the criteria enabled below.
input.use_ha_sun_night.name: Use sun position
input.use_ha_sun_night.description: This option activates night mode while the sun is below the horizon. It can be disabled if one of the two optional sensors is configured.
input.light_sensors.name: Illuminance sensors (optional)
input.light_sensors.description: Optionally, night mode can be activated when the brightness measured by the selected sensors goes below the threshold defined below. When several sensors are selected, the average is calculated automatically.
input.light_threshold.name: Illuminance threshold
input.light_threshold.description: Brightness threshold used to switch between day mode and night mode. Ignored if no illuminance sensor has been selected above.
input.night_mode_entity.name: Night mode entity (optional)
input.night_mode_entity.description: The selected input helper also activates night mode, in addition to the other active criteria.
trigger.managed_light_switched_to_color_: A light switched to color mode
